programme.title: Movie: On the Twelfth Day of Christmas

programme.title: Movie: Love You Like Christmas

programme.title: Movie: Love, Lights, Hanukkah!

programme.title: Movie: Once Upon a Christmas Miracle

programme.title: Movie: Christmas at Dollywood

programme.title: South Park

programme.desc: Just after Stan's tenth birthday, his worldviews start to change and so do his friendships.

programme.title: South Park: Ass Burgers
programme.desc: Cartman must find a way to deal with his Asperger's Syndrome.

programme.title: South Park: Cartmanland
programme.desc: Cartman inherits a million dollars from his grandmother and fulfills his lifelong dream of owning his own amusement park: Cartmanland. Meanwhile, Kyle is aghast at Cartman getting his dreams, and renounces his faith.

programme.title: South Park: Fat Camp
programme.desc: Cartman's family and friends stage an intervention, and force him to trim down. While Cartman's away, Kenny's star is on the rise when he gets his own reality TV show.

programme.title: South Park: Cartman's Incredible Gift
programme.desc: After sustaining a severe head injury, Cartman appears to have the power to see into the future. South Park detectives are quick to enlist his help in cracking unsolved murder cases.

programme.title: South Park: Tsst!
programme.desc: Cartman's mom gets help from an expert when she realizes she cannot control her son anymore. The expert, known as 'The Dog Whisperer', may have what it takes but Eric Cartman's not going down without a fight.

programme.title: South Park: Lice Capades
programme.desc: When Mrs. Garrison refuses to name names about an infestation of head lice that plagues South Park Elementary, Cartman finds a way to detect who has lice.

programme.title: South Park: The Coon
programme.desc: 'The Coon' rises from the trash and takes his place as a lone vigilante who wipes out crime in the town of South Park. When another superhero encroaches on his territory, The Coon seeks out some unlikely allies.

programme.title: South Park: ManBearPig
programme.desc: The boys are trapped in a cave and Cartman finds a treasure. Meanwhile, Al Gore shows up in town to warn the citizens about the threat of ManBearPig.

programme.title: South Park: The Death of Eric Cartman
programme.desc: Cartman is sure he's dead and Butters is sure that Eric Cartman's ghost is haunting him. Together they attempt to make amends to all the people Cartman has wronged over the years.

programme.title: South Park: Scott Tenorman Must Die
programme.desc: Cartman is excited that he has finally entered puberty when he gets pubes of his very own. After the other boys explain that pubes don't count unless you grow them, Cartman realizes he been tricked and sets in motion a dastardly plot for revenge.

programme.title: South Park: Casa Bonita
programme.desc: Kyle chooses Stan, Kenny and Butters to celebrate his birthday at Casa Bonita. When Cartman finds out he's not invited, he arranges for Butters to conveniently go 'missing.'

programme.title: South Park: Ginger Kids
programme.desc: Cartman catches the mysterious Gingervitus, a disease that turns his hair red, lightens his skin and gives him freckles. Sick of being ripped-on because of his new looks, Cartman rallies all ginger kids everywhere to fight against discrimination.

programme.title: South Park: Fat Butt and Pancake Head
programme.desc: One of Cartman's body parts becomes famous overnight and rivals the popularity of another superstar.

programme.title: South Park: Le Petit Tourette
programme.desc: Cartman discovers the joys of having Tourette's syndrome. Drunk with the power of saying whatever he wants without getting in trouble for it, he lines up National TV Coverage to take advantage of his new life with no filters.

programme.title: South Park: Are You There God? It's Me Jesus
programme.desc: On the eve of the Millennium, people from all over the world gather outside Jesus' house waiting for a miracle. Meanwhile, Kenny and Cartman plan a 'mature' New Year's Eve celebration.

programme.title: Movie: South Park: Bigger, Longer and Uncut

programme.title: South Park: Buddha Box
programme.desc: Cartman has been diagnosed with anxiety.

programme.title: Movie: Sister Act
programme.desc: A lounge singer witnesses a murder by her mobster boyfriend and hides out in a convent.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Haunting Vision
programme.desc: A woman who survived a deadly childhood fire sends police a letter charging that her father killed her mother 30 years ago.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Home Evasion
programme.desc: A husband finds his wife shot outside of their home and she mutters the name of her apparent assailant just before her death. It takes fingerprint comparison, handwriting analysis, ballistics, and DNA testing for the true killer to be revealed.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Smoking Out a Killer
programme.desc: Three college students are killed walking along a footpath over the course of six years. Police know they're dealing with a serial killer, but the hard part is catching him.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Pastoral Care
programme.desc: The body of a female prison guard shows up in a landfill and investigators suspect the hundreds of prisoners in the facility where she worked.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Voice From Beyond
programme.desc: When a decomposed body is found stuffed in a barrel, it's easy to presume murder. But the body had been placed in the barrel 30 years earlier.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Stranger in the Night
programme.desc: When his mother is found dead, a Delaware man tells a story so improbable that police don't believe it. While there's no evidence directly tying him to the crime, investigators are certain he knows more than he's saying, until forensic testing is done.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Talking Skull
programme.desc: A mapmaker was surveying the grounds of a boy scout ranch when something caught his eye. At first, it appeared to be a turtle shell, but it proved to be a human skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Dirty Little Seacret
programme.desc: A couple spent Memorial Day weekend at the beach, and then they simply vanished. A week later, police would get an unexpected lead from a robbery in progress, and the evidence they found helped solve a crime of unparalleled violence and brutality.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The List Murders
programme.desc: For 18 years, a man who murdered his entire family successfully eluded the FBI. Investigators used art and forensic science to find the killer and bring him to justice.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Bitter Potion
programme.desc: When a woman comes down with flu-like symptoms and eventually dies, a neurologist suspects she was poisoned with radioactive thallium. The FBI suspects her neighbour, who had a very extensive background in chemistry.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Lasting Impressions
programme.desc: Examine intriguing crimes, accidents and outbreaks of disease from around the world.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Good as Gold
programme.desc: Two young women are killed by a mysterious intruder. Investigators hope that his unusual brand of cigarettes and new advances in DNA technology can paint a picture of a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Disappearance of Helle Crafts
programme.desc: A flight attendant was reported missing from her home after hiring an investigator.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Holy Terror
programme.desc: When churches are bombed in a community in Illinois, the people responsible are quickly apprehended. But the bombings somehow continue, leading to this explosive investigation to be reopened.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Southside Strangler
programme.desc: Learn how investigators caught Timothy Wilson Spencer, better known as the Southside Strangler, a serial killer responsible for the murders of five women in Virginia. This was the first use of DNA profiling in the U.S. justice system.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Transaction Failed
programme.desc: When a dedicated, well-respected teacher disappeared, police had to determine if she'd gone on vacation without telling anyone, or if she was the victim of foul play. Investigators turned to forensic science, hoping to find the answers they needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shoe-In for Murder
programme.desc: A 12 year-old is the only witness to her mother's death and is unable to provide many details. Over a decade later, forensic evidence has lead the investigation right back to where it started.

programme.title: Forensic Files: All That Glitters Is Gold
programme.desc: A bullet-riddled car, a missing driver and no witnesses poses a mystery, until a tiny and unique clue is found.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Invisible Intruder
programme.desc: A Texas woman claims that an intruder attacked her and killed two of her sons, but detectives question her story when they find some inconsistencies at the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Postal Mortem
programme.desc: On October 15, 1985, two bombs rocked Salt Lake City and resulted in two deaths. A third explosion occurred the next day, but this time the victim survived. Police believed that the survivor is more than an innocent by-stander.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Woman Scorned
programme.desc: When a young mother and her infant son are found dead in a cornfield, the obvious suspect is the husband and father of the victims. But some insects found on the bodies reveal a vital clue, as does a long, blonde hair found on the victims.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Shot in the Dark
programme.desc: A woman is found dead of a gunshot wound and police conclude that she was murdered by her husband. Forensic scientists were able to piece together the clues and determine what really happened.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Elephant Tracks
programme.desc: A jeweler notices that a victim wore an elephant pendant that had been pawned in her shop.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Purebread Murder
programme.desc: A young mother is murdered after years of domestic abuse. There are clues at the scene: bloody footprints and DNA from the victim's rape kit. But the evidence which will conclusively tie the killer to the crime is on a freshly baked hamburger bun.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Haunting Vision
programme.desc: A woman who survived a deadly childhood fire sends police a letter charging that her father killed her mother 30 years ago.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Home Evasion

programme.title: Forensic Files: Southside Strangler

programme.title: Forensic Files: Transaction Failed
programme.desc: When a dedicated, well-respected teacher disappeared, police had to determine if she'd gone on vacation without telling anyone, or if she was the victim of foul play. Investigators turned to forensic science, hoping to find the answers they needed.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sunday School Ambush
programme.desc: Forensic science helps investigators unravel a twisted tale of lust, greed and deception.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Penchant for Poison
programme.desc: Three seemingly unrelated deaths proved to be serial murders. The killer had been careful; he used poison which has no taste or odor. Fortunately for investigators, it also had a unique chemical signature.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Duelling Confessions
programme.desc: When a teenage girl is found dead on the side of the road her boyfriend becomes the prime suspect. He eventually confessed to her murder, but so did another man. It would take forty years, an author and an expert in pedestrian accidents to find the truth.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Traces of Truth
programme.desc: A high school gym teacher took time off from class to give his friend a ride home -- and never returned. Investigators suspected foul play, but without a body the case would be difficult to prove.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Marked for Life
programme.desc: In 1957 the police search for a man who committed several crimes in one night, including murder. Eventually, the case turned cold. Almost fifty years later, with the help of scientific advances, the investigators find their man.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Plastic Puzzle
programme.desc: When a bicycle rider is struck and fatally injured in a hit-and-run accident, forensic investigators utilize an assortment of miniscule clues to determine the identity of the offending vehicle and eventually the person who was driving it.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Cereal Killer
programme.desc: Police must search for a boy who goes missing mysteriously after his home burns down and are disheartened when they find his body, but the killer left behind numerous clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Crash Course
programme.desc: A highway patrolman is shocked by a coroner's ruling of an 'accidental death.' after he attends a Crash scene with far more blood than usual. An anonymous tip reinvigorates the investigation.

programme.title: Serengeti: Invasion
programme.desc: The anticipation of the annual Great Migration is being felt by all of the Serengeti's families as they wait with great desperation for it to arrive. The land continues to get hotter and hunger starts to bite as food becomes even more scarce.

programme.title: Serengeti: Misfortune
programme.desc: The long-awaited arrival of the Great Migration brings opportunities and food in abundance, but it also increases conflict as every one of the Serengeti's families is put to the test.

programme.title: Serengeti: Exodus
programme.desc: The dry season turns the waterhole into a death trap for herds. But for some, this is a gift.

programme.title: Serengeti: Rebirth
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the fire, tensions are high and family members are still missing. Torrential rain brings new life, turning the scorched earth into a green paradise.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Mysteries Of The Bermuda Triangle
programme.desc: Josh searches the ocean floor and the skies for as he investigates mysteries of the Bermuda Triangle.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Revealing the Triangle
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his epic investigation, Josh Gates uses cutting-edge technology to investigate the Triangle's most infamous shipwreck and lost flights. Then, Expedition X probes America's most haunted lake and an extraterrestrial cult in Thailand.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Nazis in Argentina
programme.desc: Josh ventures to Argentina, where many Nazi SS officers escaped to after World War II.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: The Secret Solved
programme.desc: Breaking news forces Josh to race across the globe. One of the wickedly hard puzzles of The Secret, a case Josh has been chasing for years, might have been solved. He's been crisscrossing the US to track it down, but this solve is in Josh's hometown.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Mystery of Dead Mountain
programme.desc: Braving the sub-zero conditions of Siberia, Josh Gates investigates one of the strangest and most brutal cold cases of all-time: the Dyatlov Pass Incident. Nine Soviet hikers died suspiciously. Sixty years later, no one knows how or why.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Siberia's Coldest Case
programme.desc: Josh Gates concludes his in-depth investigation of the Dyatlov Pass Incident. Taking on Dead Mountain's brutal conditions, Josh and two investigators re-trace the last steps of the hikers. A big discovery could finally solve the mystery.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: India's Atlantis
programme.desc: Josh Gates travels to India in search of Dwarka, a mythical kingdom swallowed by the ocean. To find evidence that the golden city is real, Josh dives into the Arabian Sea and visits a dig site that could rewrite history.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Search for the Sultan's Heart
programme.desc: Four years after Josh Gates went on a journey to find the lost heart of Sultan Suleiman the Magnificent, he travels to the newly rediscovered town of Turbek for some shocking new revelations about the case.

programme.title: Growing Belushi: A Mission from God
programme.desc: Jim and Chris are on their way to Idaho for a Blues Brothers show with Dan Aykroyd, where Jim plans to ask Dan for the right to use the Blues Brothers' brand. Dan sets Jim on a 'Mission from God' to Colombia, in pursuit of elusive cannabis strains.

programme.title: Growing Belushi: The Weed Brothers
programme.desc: After losing the seeds of two prized Colombian strains, Jim and Chris are back at square one. The cousins explore Pablo Escobar's old haunts while trying to salvage their trip. Back home, they celebrate the harvest of Jim's strains with a big party.

programme.title: Moonshiners: No Ordinary Run
programme.desc: Tickle and the Laws unleash a 700-gallon run on a whisper-quiet steamer still. Mark and Huck traverse Appalachia's roughest terrain to run liquor with moonshine legend Jim Tom. Mike and Jerry engineer a high-tech solution to a backwoods problem.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Single Malt Moonshine
programme.desc: Mark and Digger's plan to employ an out of work friend backfires on payday. Tim invents a low-cost way to make his first single-malt moonshine. With their backwoods column still cranking out liquor, Mike discovers Jerry isn't carrying his own weight.

programme.title: Moonshiners: Master Distiller: High Proof Cherry Bounce
programme.desc: Legal and outlaw distillers compete to make a 300-year-old spirit called Cherry Bounce, a favorite drink of George Washington, nearly lost to time. Josh Owens, whose family kept the recipe alive for generations in the backwoods, joins as guest judge.

programme.title: Gold Rush: The Perfect Storm
programme.desc: As gold prices spike to record highs and the cost of fuel plummets, Parker, Rick and Tony race to take advantage of the perfect conditions. But after the worldwide lockdown delayed the start of the season, there's less time than ever to cash in.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Promised Land
programme.desc: Parker expands to a second cut and puts his trust in a rookie foreman. Tony battles the cursed cut. Rick fires up his plant on rich ground. Greenhorn Fred Lewis calls in a mining legend. The miners reveal their secrets for striking it rich gold mining.

programme.title: Gold Rush: The Fast and The Furious
programme.desc: Parker's new strategy to build a war chest creates an engineering challenge, igniting a flare up with his foremen. Rick pulls in his first gold of the season. Fred calls in reinforcements.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Breaking the Piggy Bank
programme.desc: Tony gambles on a hidden strip of paydirt. Parker's crew works overtime to run both wash plants in hope of a big payoff. Rick scrambles to keep is small crew running.

programme.title: Gold Rush: It's Alive
programme.desc: Rick bets nearly all his gold on a new machine that could transform his operation. Tony resurrects an abandoned secret weapon. Parker hires new crew members to make up for lost time.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Pillow Talk: Judgement Day
programme.desc: Tania and Syngin struggle with communication. Juliana seeks independence. Religion divides Mike and Natalie. Mursel makes an important decision. Jasmin finds it hard to fit in. Anny's birthday gets wild. Angela hears news about Michael's visa.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Pillow Talk: I Don't Have a Choice
programme.desc: Anna reveals big news to her family. Angela fights for Michael's visa. Syngin needs more from Tania. Mike has tough questions for Natalie. Betsy grills Sasha about his past. Anny cries shopping for a wedding dress. Blake's mom tries to bond with Jasmin.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Pillow Talk: Choose Me
programme.desc: Mursel packs his bags for Turkey. Angela arrives in Nigeria. Natalie's reveal leaves Mike devastated. Juliana bonds with Michael's ex. Tania and Syngin's romantic reunion goes south. Blake's friends confront him about Jasmin.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Pillow Talk: Blindsided
programme.desc: Blake and Jasmin argue on a night out. Mike returns to Washington. Emily and Betsy shop for a wedding dress. Michael's family confront Angela about their wedding. Tania's revelation pushes Syngin away. It's Michael and Juliana's wedding day.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Pillow Talk: I Do and I Don't Know
programme.desc: Syngin considers going back to South Africa. Mursel feels he made a mistake. Mike consults a lawyer. Angela rethinks a Nigerian wedding. Jasmin and Blake's families meet. Stephanie makes Anny an offer.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Pillow Talk: Can I Get a Witness
programme.desc: Blake and Jasmin, Anna and Mursel, Robert and Anny, and Tania and Syngin are out of time and must wed. Angela and Michael have a setback. Mike's call to Natalie leaves some unanswered questions. Michael talks to his ex-wife about his prenup decision.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Pillow Talk: Tell All Part 1
programme.desc: The couples meet for the first time in a dramatic showdown in New York City. Host Shaun Robinson gets answers to your burning questions as shocking reveals and juicy details are uncovered from this season.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Pillow Talk: Tell All Part 2
programme.desc: The couples of 90 Day Fiancé meet for the first time in a dramatic Tell All showdown in New York City. Host Shaun Robinson gets answers to your burning questions as shocking reveals and juicy details are uncovered from this season.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Love Can't Wait
programme.desc: Ed, Avery, Geoffrey, Lisa, Stephanie, Yolanda and David are on a mission to meet their overseas soulmates in person for the very first time. Nothing can stop them from seeing if their dreams of love can become reality, and Darcey returns for some answers.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Great Expectations
programme.desc: Darcey decodes mixed messages from Tom. Ed packs gifts for his queen. Yolanda has a confusing conversation with Williams. Geoffrey begins his journey to Russia. Avery seeks her parent's approval and Lisa's friends are concerned for her safety.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Risky Business
programme.desc: Stephanie makes a life-changing decision. Lisa meets her Nigerian fiancé. Ed worries Rose won't feel a connection. Darcey makes a shocking discovery. Geoffrey's first day in Russia is off to a bumpy start. Yolanda has concerns about Williams social media.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Baby Be Mine
programme.desc: Usman surprises Lisa. Darcey faces the truth. Avery & Ash finally meet. Stephanie puts her health at risk to travel to Australia. David makes plans to go to Ukraine. Geoffrey questions Varya's intentions and Ed worries about Rose and her family's motives.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Our Lips Are Sealed
programme.desc: Avery meets Ash's skeptical brother. Ed makes a shocking request. Geoffrey's biggest fear about Varya comes true. Lisa reaches a boiling point. Yolanda hopes she isn't making a mistake. David takes a leap of faith, and Stephanie and Erika finally meet.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Can't Buy Me Love
programme.desc: Darcey and Tom prepare to meet face to face. Geoffrey questions Varya and the entire trip to Moscow. David anxiously awaits Lana. Stephanie and Erika get romantic. Ed faces regret, while Lisa faces Sojaboy's adoring fans at his music video premiere.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Breaking the Bank
programme.desc: Alexis has searched everywhere for a certain gown, only to now find it's double her budget. Jenna's dream dress turns into a nightmare when it's $7,000 over budget. Lauren is committed to going over budget on her see-through bodice.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: V.I.Pnina
programme.desc: Sometimes when Kleinfeld is closed the salon will specially open the doors for clients with unlimited budgets to have private appointments with their designers. Pnina Tornai meets individually with three budget-less brides for a record-breaking day.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: You ARE a Daredevil!
programme.desc: Paralympic snowboarder Amy Purdy isn't afraid to take risks, like waiting to find her dress just three weeks before the big day. Katie is the ultimate planner and brings a list ranking her top gowns.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: That Looks Like a Torture Device
programme.desc: Bridget's feelings about the perfect dress for her wedding to pro snowboarder Scott Lago get turned upside-down by her opinionated entourage. Meanwhile, new mom Karyssa wants a dress with lots of coverage and Lauren hopes she fits into her gown.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Who's Crying Now
programme.desc: Darcey and Tom finally meet face to face and Yolanda investigates Williams. Ed meets Rosemarie's family while Stephanie and Erika get crafty. Varya's mother questions Geoffrey and Avery and Ash take a romantic getaway.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Stranger in a Strange Land
programme.desc: Usman asks for his mother's blessing. Varya shows Geoffrey her hometown. Ed spends the night at Rosemarie's. Ash and Avery tour the outback. David continues his quest for Lana. Stephanie travels to Erika's hometown, and Darcey and Tom process everything.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Should've Known Better
programme.desc: Lisa and Usman plead their case to get married. Stephanie meets Erika's friends. Avery questions Ash's professional relationships. David prepared to meet Lana. Yolanda updates Karra on Williams. Geoffrey opens up to Varya's mom, and Darcey gets focused.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Cuts Both Ways
programme.desc: Darcey has a surprise visitor, David doesn't give up on Lana and Ed confronts Rose about her sister. Avery attends Ash's seminar, Stephanie and Erika try to reconnect and Geoffrey and Varya go camping. Plus, LIsa and Usman wait for a decision.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Private Eyes
programme.desc: Lisa and Usman find common ground while Ed and Rosemarie enjoy some alone time. Later, Stephanie meets Erika's parents and Geoffrey and Varya have a spa day. Plus, Darcey says goodbye, while David plans an investigation.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: King of Wishful Thinking
programme.desc: Avery spends some time with Ash's ex. Ed and Rose discuss their relationship. Erika opens up to Stephanie about her past. David listens to his heart, and Geoffrey and Varya look to the future.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: The Pleasure Principle
programme.desc: Geoffrey settles back home. Lisa confronts Usman. Ed leaves the Philippines. Avery and Ash spend time with his son. Darcey rethinks her priorities. Stephanie and Erika say goodbye. Yolanda wants answers. David continues his quest for Lana.

programme.title: 90 Day Fiancé: Before the 90 Days: Pillow Talk: Hard Habit To Break
programme.desc: David tries to get closer to Lana. Avery says goodbye to Ash. Geoffrey finds himself caught in the middle of a sticky situation. Ed returns home. Stephanie reunites with her mom, and drama ensues when Lisa meets with Usman's elders.

programme.desc: Detectives Mike McCleery and Bob Bachelder investigate the murder of a promising high-school senior, shot to death the week before Christmas, and stuffed in the trunk of his car, 25 miles from home.

programme.title: The First 48: Shattered Glass
programme.desc: In New Orleans, when a young mother is found beaten to death in her home, Detective Greg Johnson must fight to prove that he has enough evidence against her killer.

programme.title: The First 48: Dark Waters
programme.desc: New Orleans Detective Ryan Vaught takes on his most challenging case yet when a young married couple goes missing and one of them is found floating in a river, drowned to death.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Bidder Loser
programme.desc: The guys ask strangers to help them bury bizarre items, and then they try to cling onto strangers’ cars. The big loser makes enemies at an auction house.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: X Man
programme.desc: The guys compete in their first four-way challenge. Later, see what happens when they try to lead a kid's art class, and which loser fails so badly that he's hit with an on-the-spot punishment that leaves him seeing red.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Butt of the Joker
programme.desc: The guys pair up in focus group presentations, and tonight's loser tries to cure the public of a nasty habit, but gets people smoking mad.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Stuffed Turkey
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Joe and Murr act as not-so-handy-men assembling furniture. Thanksgiving comes early for tonight's loser when he must talk turkey at a dating event.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Mime and Punishment
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Murr and Q put the nasty in namaste when they lead guided meditations. Tonight's big loser gets boxed in with a 24-hour punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dover and Out
programme.desc: The guys survey employees with odd questions, and tonight's loser unveils a mural in a small town whose residents react with big city rage.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Captain Fatbelly
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Murr and Q lend an unhelpful hand while working at a high-tech store, then play detectives at the mall in search of perps on the loose. Tonight's loser gets the shakes while soaring high above the city.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Stripped of Dignity
programme.desc: The guys get a taste of babysitting while asking reluctant strangers to watch their kid, then head to the grocery store to conduct taste(less) tests with shoppers, and tonight's loser is forced to strip for the wrong people.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Stare Master
programme.desc: The guys play a game of copycat at the park while babysitting kids, and then hit the supermarket for a game of catch and release.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Wrapper's Delight
programme.desc: The guys make a mockery of each other while acting as restaurant managers, sing along with strangers trying to name that (made up) tune. The loser gets wrapped up in a birthday surprise that definitely wasn't on his wish list.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Parent Trap
programme.desc: The guys get stuck in a grocery post-it showdown, and tonight's big loser earns a time-out for his outrageous behavior at a kids' play space.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Take Me Out at the Ballgame
programme.desc: The guys get drenched at a water park, and tonight's loser strikes out while jockeying for a ball from Mets pitcher Noah Syndergaard.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Catastrophe
programme.desc: The guys serve up the goods at a juice bar, and tonight's loser confronts his biggest fear in a punishment that leaves him practically catatonic.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Medium, Well Done
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Q, and Joe pose as TV execs pitching insane shows, and tonight's big loser undergoes a psychic reading that puts his real life on the wrong track.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Three Men and Your Baby
programme.desc: Sal, Joe, Murr, and Q get snap happy posing as photographers, and all but one of the guys are faced with a punishment that's not child's play.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Flatfoot the Pirate
programme.desc: Q, Sal, Joe and Murr explain why they've been doused with water at the mall, and tonight's big loser plays a pirate but won't find any rum in his cup.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Silence of the Lame
programme.desc: It's all play and no work when the guys square off in a toy-pitching competition. And tonight's big loser has to quiet down patrons in a food court.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Walking Dread
programme.desc: The guys take time to smell the roses, then pair up to double dip at the mall. Tonight's loser battles an undead enemy to save his family.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Needy and the Greedy
programme.desc: A behind the scenes look at the location that refused to let the Jokers film, Joe's injury from being thrown into a tower of cereal and why Q was called a 'loose cannon'.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: To Hatch a Predator
programme.desc: A last-minute prop request that caused a scramble, where Murr’s fetching housecoat came from, and who was offered a job at the children’s museum following Q’s turtle punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Out of Left Field
programme.desc: Find out whose familiar face showed up in the waiting room, how many dogs Sal had to babysit, and which Joker came up with the punishment that left Joe feeling numb.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Butterfly Crime Scene
programme.desc: Find out what classic phrase was uttered in this early episode that is still said today, and what unaired challenges almost made it to the show. Plus, the real backstory of Butterfly Crime Scene.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Drawing a Blank
programme.desc: Find out what classic bit began in this early episode, which Jokers used to live together, and why filming in Times Square became a bad idea.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Out of TP
programme.desc: Find out why so many early-season challenges happened in a mall, how the team hid the cameras, and what Sal said that became an instant classic. Plus, what the crew considers to be one of the first iconic moments of the show.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Bro, They're Surrounding Us
programme.desc: Michael reveals an amazing credit card hack, demonstrates how far stem cell research has come, and spooks a gnome-fearing gardener.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: It's a Chicken, Sir
programme.desc: Michael discovers a ferocious new breed of animal at a park, invades a local tea shop, and transforms himself at an abandoned art studio.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Stripteased
programme.desc: Find out what prop Q asked to keep from the dental damn challenge, what glazed delight is known to give Joe great power and how a doctor's appointment inspired Murr's striptease punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Turning the Tables
programme.desc: Find out how the Jokers keep their cover while filming at the mall, and Q's favorite VR word. Plus, learn how long Joe was sore after the table-busting punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: No Child Left Behind
programme.desc: Find out how you make a 'Grandpa Whoopsie' doll, why winter is the perfect season to play pin for the win and where the idea for Q's dirty diaper challenge came from.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Bull Shiatsu
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes to find out what word Joe uses as slang for cash, which prop did Q have to handle with care and how Joe managed to keep cool during his chair punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Washed Up
programme.desc: How the phrase “rooty toot toot” became popular on set, which guy has recently cut his potato intake in half, and how Joe managed to cool off during his sweltering bikini car wash punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Hump Day
programme.desc: Learn how the crew attracted more customers to eat at GRK, what piece of iconic 'Jokers' history was made in this same focus group space and who caused several delays in the filming of Q's zoo punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Hell on Wheels
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes and discover what kind of vehicle the Jokers had to stay in for a challenge. Plus, find out how Murr’s TV career actually started. Finally, find out why tonight's punishment was nostalgic for the guys.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Pay It Forward
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes on the very first Impractical Jokers episode and discover what social superpower the Jokers now possess. Plus, find out which audio equipment is not used anymore and the true history around Joe's 'nosing'.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Dark Side of the Moon
programme.desc: The guys make passes at supermarket shoppers, then pose as reporters. Then, with a painfully invasive punishment, tonight's loser is the butt of the joke.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Ash Clown
programme.desc: The guys give customers an eye opening experience at a glasses store, and convince customers to pass illicit, and ridiculous, messages while shopping for groceres.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Three Men and Your Baby

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Q-Pay
programme.desc: The guys kill time in a waiting room by sharing some TMI texts with strangers, then try to sabotage each other as the wingmen from hell at a dating event. Tonight's big loser gets a very special donation from one of the other Jokers.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Remember the Pact
programme.desc: Murr, Sal, Joe and Q beg shoppers to hold on to a weird item, then teach tech to seniors. Tonight's loser reveals his embarrassing digital life to his dad.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: The Party Crasher
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q and Murr dodge shakedowns with the help of some strangers, and tonight's loser has a run-in at a party that leaves him down for the count.

programme.desc: A sexy con artist preys on Delinda's sympathy and scams the daughter of Big Ed for cash.

programme.title: Las Vegas: Luck Be a Lady

programme.title: Las Vegas: Year of the Tiger

programme.desc: When one half of a magic troop vanishes, Danny and Ed search for the missing sorceress.

programme.title: Las Vegas: Blood and Sand

programme.title: Las Vegas: Hellraisers and Heartbreakers
programme.desc: Ed's old CIA comrade visits the casino pitching a new security system for the hotel.

programme.desc: Sin City screeches to a halt as a massive power outage brings darkness to the strip.

programme.desc: When a 'whale' skips town owing almost five million dollars, Ed has 36 hours to find it.

programme.desc: Montecito's guest wins, but his wallet is stolen before he can indulge in his winnings.

programme.desc: Falling in love with a new employee, Nessa presses her new mystery man for more details.

programme.title: Las Vegas: Family Jewels
programme.desc: When five million dollars of diamonds are stolen, Danny and Mike try to catch the thief.

programme.title: Las Vegas: The Big Bang
programme.desc: Nessa discovers that a distraught blackjack player has a bomb strapped to his chest.

programme.title: Las Vegas: Always Faithful

programme.title: Las Vegas: The Count of Montecito
programme.desc: Nessa is reunited with Fred Puterbaugh who professes his love and admiration for her.

programme.title: Las Vegas: Blood Is Thicker
programme.desc: Sam, Nessa, Mary and Delinda desperately attempt to find tenants for their new joint-home.

programme.title: Las Vegas: Catch of the Day
programme.desc: A stolen truckload of lobsters throws a wrench into Sam's plan for an elite wedding.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Wings 'n Things
programme.desc: Guy goes to a Mexican restaurant that tosses chicken wings with homemade adobo sauce.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Signature Sandwiches
programme.desc: Guy is visiting places across the country and trying out their sandwiches.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: One Street Wonders
programme.desc: This trip, Guy is heading to Cincinnati to try three righteous restaurants all on the same street. He checks out The Funky Pub where they're serving up all kinds of hot dogs and sammies.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Succulent Sandwiches
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's diggin' into succulent sandwiches! In Oakland, a real-deal butchery stacking up killer cheesesteak and off-the-chain chicken.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: From Big Burgers To Little Italy
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri's going from the bomb burgers to a taste of Italy. In Little Rock, Ark., a funky joint pairing their craft brews with all kinds of on-the-bun creations.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Beef It Up
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's divin' into menus boasting the bomb beef.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: A Festival of Flavor
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's pullin' over for the bounty of the county.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Chicken and Briskets
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri's digging into everything from chicken to brisket. In Fargo, N.D., a funky place piling barbecue onto mac and throwin' heat into wings. A Mexican joint in Parkland, Fla., pumping out righteous recipes inside a gas station.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Legends of Texas
programme.desc: Guy is cruising the lone star state for a bite of Texas style triple d. From gumbo to Greek, pizza to burgers, and tasty twists from Dallas to Houston.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Far Out
programme.desc: Guy hones in on spots that are doing it different in Syracuse and in Hoboken.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Culinary Journey
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's hitting the road for on an off-the-chain culinary journey. On the Big Island, a Filipino-Hawaiian mashup making a name for leaving diners speechless.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Creole, Cold Cuts and Crepes
programme.desc: Guy's cookin' up everything from creole to cold cuts to crazy Asian crepes. First, a multi-generational joint bringing New Orleans to Los Angeles with off-the-hook gumbo and etouffee.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Finger Lickin' Food
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri is eating up grub that doesn't require a knife and fork! In Atlanta, a funky old house turned brew pub cookin' up cheddarwurst corn dogs along with a knockout pastrami sandwich.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: East Coast Comfort
programme.desc: Guy is making stops up and down the east coast for some home cooking. He checks out a Southern comfort join in Cambridge, Mass., pierogi in Fall River, and meatballs and fries in Providence.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Grillin', Chillin' and Huli Huli Chicken
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy is heading to unique grills on the mainland. The first stop is a husband a wife duo serving up classic Americana with a 21st century twist.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Old Standards, New Styles
programme.desc: This trip, Guy is hitting up spots serving comfort food that has been turned up a notch. In Long Beach, CA., and old school diner with a new chef is cooking some home-style classics.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Isle-Talian
programme.desc: Guy's diving into an island-Italian mashup. In Plymouth, Mass., a colourful joint dishes out a unique plantain sandwich and wings gone bananas. In Colorado Springs, a neighbourhood Italian spot serves up outrageous arancini and real-deal veal.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Multitaskers
programme.desc: A Greek diner is decked out in neon and servin' up family recipes like Yia Yia's grape leaves. In Oxnard, Calif., the jack of all trades joint is baking, brewing and cooking all in the same restaurant.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Belly Up
programme.desc: A Chicago brewhouse serves up beer to go with mussels, a brisket sandwich and headcheese.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Brain Freeze
programme.desc: Guy Fieri rolls out for ice cream, Triple D style: With visits to a Norfolk, Va. Drive-In for an original waffle cone, an Arizona creamery making Italian gelato in all flavours and a New Orleans favourite doing huge sundaes with 400 flavours.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Wings, Dogs and Claws
programme.desc: Guy checks out the best homemade cooking in the country.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Hometown Heroes
programme.desc: Guy us diving deep into beloved neighborhoods across the country to check out some local hot spots.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Roasting on an Open Fire
programme.desc: Alex Guarnaschelli and magician Penn Jillette are hoping for a Christmas miracle, beating Bobby Flay. Adil Fawzi and Ariane Duarte faceoff in the hopes of receiving the best gift of all, a win over Bobby.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Back to School
programme.desc: It's a schoolyard battle of all-star pastry chefs as Bobby Flay's friend, the legendary Francois Payard, takes on the whimsical creations of top New York City pastry chef, Zac Young.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Sicilian and Seafood

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Globe Tasting
programme.desc: This trip, Guy's getting an authentic taste of all ends of the earth. In Bakersfield, Calif., the firehouse-turned-restaurant firing up Latin-fusion favorites like basque lamb tacos and popcorn pork with a side of zing.

programme.desc: Guy is filling up on some soup. In Baltimore, the authentic Irish pub is serving seafood gumbo and a twist on traditional corned beef and cabbage.

programme.desc: On this trip Guy digs into killer grub like Michigan-made pizza pie and Philly sandwiches.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: BBQ Road Show
programme.desc: Guy is tucking in the napkin and getting into some righteous barbecue. In Dallas, the barbecue stand tucked into a farmers' market is dishing out classic beef brisket and Carolina pork.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Cookin' It Old School
programme.desc: Guy Fieri uncovers three joints putting' a piece of history on the table: In New Orleans, an oyster bar where nothing's changed since 1919 and the deep fried oysters are still hand-shucked.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives: Loaded, Stuffed and Fried
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's hitting the road and digging into joints that are loading it, stuffing it and frying it. In Santa Fe, N.M., a farm-to-table gem putting out hearty goodness like bison gravy fries and a lights-out lamb sandwich.

programme.desc: When Carrie insists that Doug join an overeaters support group, he secretly opts out.

programme.desc: At Doug's encouragement, Carrie reluctantly agrees to start taking pole dancing lessons, but quickly starts to enjoy the classes and begins feeling better about herself. However, when Carrie puts on a show for him, he is shocked by how badly she dances.

programme.title: The King of Queens: G'Night Stalker
programme.desc: When Doug starts receiving fan mail from someone who saw his performance at a karaoke club, he lets the attention go to his head, only to have his ego shattered when the emails suddenly stop.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Knee Jerk

programme.desc: When the Heffernans receive an oil painting of themselves as an anniversary gift from Deacon and Kelly, they dislike the picture so much that they decide to stage a robbery rather than hang the painting in their home.

programme.desc: From Canada's waterlogged wilderness comes stories of a 20-foot lake monster that attacks humans. Freshwater detective Jeremy Wade tries to track down this shadowy predator with a mouth said to resemble a bear-trap.

programme.title: River Monsters: Alaska's Cold Water Killer
programme.desc: For years fishermen have been vanishing in Alaska's remote and unforgiving wilderness, their deaths unseen, their bodies never found. For freshwater detective Jeremy Wade, a recent fatal incident sends him on a search for possible suspects.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Jazznastics
programme.desc: Christo gets caught in the middle of a debate between Fred and Dustin Hurt on who started the conflict in camp. Later, Fred Lewis and his crew do their best to keep morale high despite low gold totals, an easier job thanks to new Dirt favorite Johnny.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Never Say Die
programme.desc: With the arctic winter looming, the crew races against the clock. Rain and thawing snow flood the mine and Jack puts his life on the line as the glory hole caves in around the massive 100,000 pound excavator.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Virgin Ground
programme.desc: In a desperate bid to salvage their mining season, the Hoffman crew head to the Klondike. Dakota Fred fills in holes at Porcupine Creek.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Family Feud
programme.desc: Jack and Todd try to figure out how to set up their new claim. Fred finds gold that was left behind in the wash plant.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Slippery Slope
programme.desc: Todd invests five days and $5,000 to clear new flatter ground, only to discover the area is already mined out. And rival miner, Dakota Fred, still hasn't found gold at Porcupine Creek but the arrival of his earth-crunching de-rocker could save the season.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Drill or Die
programme.desc: A crew rebellion forces Todd Hoffman to dig test holes on the claim and the tension mounts as they wait to see if they're sitting on a gold-rich pay streak or about to go bust. Fred is forced to abandon mining as floods in North Dakota destroy his home.

programme.desc: In a desperate bid to salvage their mining season, the Hoffman crew head to the Klondike. Dakota Fred fills in holes at Porcupine Creek.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Family Feud
programme.desc: Jack and Todd try to figure out how to set up their new claim. Fred finds gold that was left behind in the wash plant.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Slippery Slope
programme.desc: Todd invests five days and $5,000 to clear new flatter ground, only to discover the area is already mined out. And rival miner, Dakota Fred, still hasn't found gold at Porcupine Creek but the arrival of his earth-crunching de-rocker could save the season.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Drill or Die
programme.desc: A crew rebellion forces Todd Hoffman to dig test holes on the claim and the tension mounts as they wait to see if they're sitting on a gold-rich pay streak or about to go bust. Fred is forced to abandon mining as floods in North Dakota destroy his home.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Life on the Border
programme.desc: Up in the North Woods along the Canadian border, things are very different. A warden looking for smugglers encounters a drunk driver with a gun, and a suicidal man enters the woods with a shotgun.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Lost and Found
programme.desc: A new class of wardens are being trained to do search and rescues. A hiker vanishes on the Appalachian trail and the wardens lead one of the largest search and rescue operations in Maine's history to find her.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Most Memorable Rescues
programme.desc: The officers revisit their most memorable rescues. Sgt. Lucas reflects on an intense rescue mission on Mt. Madison. MacFadzen recounts the rescue of a barred owl trapped in a fence. And Bronson recalls a multi-day search for a missing hiker.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Bait and Switch
programme.desc: McKee and Pushee confront two hunters lying about illegally baiting deer, Officer Arsenault deals with a tight-lipped teenage fisherman who refuses to provide any information and Lt. Boudreau stumbles upon a couple in a compromising position.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Cold Case
programme.desc: Officers Bronson and Lucas help state police investigate a 19-year-old missing person case. A landowner requests help with a group of squatters on an island in the Connecticut River. And, Officer Towne comes to the aid of an injured hawk.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Leading the Way
programme.desc: Sgt. Pushee leads a multi-agency rescue mission to find a missing kayaker. A specialist is called in to locate a pesky bear roaming a residential area, and a boy is caught riding without a helmet and operating his ATV illegally.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Occupational Hazards
programme.desc: Deep in the Maine woods, danger lurks behind every tree for the game wardens. Warden Herring comes across a sprawling illegal encampment on private property. Warden Stevens discovers three marijuana grows and must seize dozens of plants.

programme.title: North Woods Law: In a Hot Second
programme.desc: On land and water, it's the wardens' job to keep people safe. Warden McBrine coordinates a LifeFlight helicopter for the victim of a serious car crash. Warden Buuck spreads the word on boating safety.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Close Encounters of the Wild Kind
programme.desc: When Maine's wildlife wanders into human territory, it's game wardens to the rescue. Wardens Marquis and Fitzpatrick try to catch a dumpster-diving bear. A wily woodchuck gives Warden Lounder a run for his money.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Can't Believe Your Eyes
programme.desc: Whether it's protecting wildlife or ensuring public safety, the Maine Game Wardens are on double duty during the summer. Warden Jeremy Judd tries to rescue a baby skunk in distress. Wardens MacCabe and Robertson investigate a stolen ATV that disappeared.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Taking the Bait
programme.desc: Officer Masucci and Major Walsh join forces to summon a man for baiting without a permit. Officer Bronson pursues a pair of ATV speedsters riding with a toddler with no safety gear, and Lieutenant Cheney responds to a call about a deer wedged in a fence.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Small Town Troubles
programme.desc: Game wardens are ready for springtime in Texas. Wardens investigate a video of an illegal hunter. A concerned citizen reports a hunter on his property. A fisherman is illegally catching oysters. A game warden smells something fishy going on.

programme.title: House of Payne: Firestorm
programme.desc: A routine fire becomes C.J.'s worst nightmare. Meanwhile, Ella recommends a book to Miranda and Claretha and finds out why it's such a hot seller.

programme.title: House of Payne: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Calvin and Miranda have a pregnancy scare. Meanwhile, Curtis and Claretha team up to make some quick cash.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: It Had to Be You

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Nice Lady
programme.desc: Will tries to prove that he's a responsible person so he can take the car to Nevada.

programme.title: Black-ish: Things Were Different Then

programme.title: Black-ish: North Star
programme.desc: When both Dre and Bow's families show up for Easter, they have to learn to love each other's different cuisines. Meanwhile, Junior tries to organize an Easter egg hunt but Jack and Diane pretend not to be interested to impress their cooler cousins.

programme.title: Martin: The Bachelor Party
programme.desc: Martin and Gina worry at their bachelor parties, and imagine what life will be like in five years.

programme.title: Martin: Wedding Bell Blues

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion: Man vs. Snake
programme.desc: Troy Landry, his son Chase, Bruce Mitchell, Zak Bagby and Bill Booth are called on to combat an invasion of Burmese pythons.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion: The Everglades Triangle
programme.desc: One week into the hunt, the swampers venture into the Everglades Triangle. Bruce, Zak and Bill hunt near an abandoned rocket silo, while Troy and Chase scour a cattle ranch for an African Rock Python.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion: Taking Back the Glades

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion: Rise of the Super Snakes

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion: Skunk Ape Alley

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion: Venom Warriors
programme.desc: Troy, Chase and Bill dive into water to catch pythons but may be in over their heads. Meanwhile, Bruce and Zak venture into Viper Jungle, an area filled with venomous snakes.

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion: Return to the Everglades Triangle
programme.desc: The hunters venture into the Everglade Triangles, known for its unexplained disappearances and swarms of snakes.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Satan Conspiracy
programme.desc: New interpretations of ancient texts indicate that Satan may have been an extraterrestrial who was more of an ally to humanity than an enemy by passing along knowledge that planted the seed for civilization.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Vanishings
programme.desc: Stories of civilizations large and small disappearing without a trace can be found throughout history and across the globe.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Desert Codes
programme.desc: Giant ancient drawings found etched into the desert floor could be part of an ancient alien code.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: Return to Mars
programme.desc: As humanity prepares to colonize beyond Earth, we examine whether we are simply realizing humanity's future or returning to an extra-terrestrial past.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: They Came From the Sea
programme.desc: Lurking in our oceans are creatures that defy explanation. Octopuses can edit their own DNA, animals are able to regenerate their entire brains, and jellyfish can literally live forever. Learn is they were delivered to Earth by extraterrestrials.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Immortality Machine
programme.desc: In the Mojave Desert, there is a structure built to bestow everlasting life, and according to the architect, the blueprint was provided by an extraterrestrial visitor.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Galactic Keyhole
programme.desc: The symbol of the keyhole exists in ancient texts, architecture and artwork. It could be a profound connection to the cosmos and a clue left behind by extraterrestrials.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Lost Kingdom
programme.desc: According to the earliest Tibetan traditions, the kingdom of the gods Shambhala is hidden high in the Himalaya Mountains.

programme.title: Jessie: Jessie's Big Break

programme.desc: Jessie returns to Texas with the Ross kids in tow, excited to show them the military base where she was raised and introduce them to her father, only to learn the surprising news that her dad is engaged to be married to her high school rival's mother.

programme.title: Jessie: G.I. Jessie
programme.desc: Jessie returns to Texas with the Ross kids in tow, excited to show them the military base where she was raised and introduce them to her father, only to learn the surprising news that her dad is engaged to be married to her high school rival's mother.

programme.desc: Austin, Ally, Trish and Dez travel to Times Square where Austin is booked to perform on New Year's Eve. When they can't get through New York City's crowded streets, Jessie comes up with a plan to get them on stage and have Austin record one of her songs.
